message:
feat(net): implement AsyncReadVectored for TCP and Unix stream split halves

Adds poll_read_vectored support to both borrowed (ReadHalf) and owned
(OwnedReadHalf) read halves for TCP and Unix streams, enabling
scatter-gather reads through the async interface. This complements the
existing AsyncWriteVectored support added in a prior commit.

TCP split halves include both native (non-wasm32) implementations that
delegate to std::io::Read::read_vectored with WouldBlock → Pending
translation, and wasm32 stubs that return Unsupported. Unix split halves
only need native implementations since Unix sockets are not available on
wasm32.

The OwnedReadHalf variants properly register reactor interest on
WouldBlock, while the borrowed ReadHalf variants use the fallback_rewake
path consistent with the existing AsyncRead implementations.

Tests added for both borrowed and owned TCP split read halves using a
helper that reads a 6-byte payload across two IoSliceMut buffers,
verifying correct scatter-gather behavior across chunk boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
